summary: Frontend-only test of Convoy v4 Google Maps integration. Login works, map screen renders ConvoyMap.web component with @vis.gl/react-google-maps APIProvider and Map mounted, Google Maps JS API script is requested with the provided key, top header (Map title, '0 drivers · 0 alerts', refresh-btn) renders, report-fab and VoiceFAB are positioned above the tab bar without overlap. HOWEVER the map itself FAILS to render Google tiles because the API key returns 'ApiNotActivatedMapError' from Google. As a result no .gm-style is created, no tiles, no markers, and the screen shows Google's 'Oops! Something went wrong. This page didn't load Google Maps correctly.' fallback. A secondary AdvancedMarker crash ('Cannot read properties of undefined (reading get)') is triggered by the same root cause + mapId not being a registered Cloud Map ID (AdvancedMarker requires a vector map with a valid Cloud Map ID). The dev-mode LogBox red error overlay blocks all subsequent UI interactions (report panel, tab bar), so the report-police -> marker flow and other-tab regressions could not be validated.

ui_bugs.issue: ApiNotActivatedMapError - the Google Maps JavaScript API is NOT enabled in the Google Cloud project for key AIzaSyDLysQuU6tOcmbdlsHJPYUqviq4TrwEUx0. Map renders Google's grey error fallback ('This page didn't load Google Maps correctly.') instead of hybrid satellite tiles.

Runtime TypeError 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading get)' inside set position of AdvancedMarker. AdvancedMarker requires (a) Maps JS API enabled and (b) a valid Cloud-registered Map ID for vector rendering. mapId='convoy-driver-map' is not registered in Google Cloud, so even after enabling the API the markers may still crash unless a real Map ID is created (or AdvancedMarker is replaced with the classic Marker for raster fallback).

tilt={45} and colorScheme=DARK are silently ignored because the mapId is not a valid Cloud Map ID -> Google falls back to a raster map, which logs 'CameraOptions includes tilt or heading, which are not supported on raster maps' and 'satellite/hybrid no longer automatically switch to 45° imagery' (deprecation since v3.62).

integration_issues.issue: Could not validate. report-fab is present and tappable, but the LogBox red error overlay (caused by the AdvancedMarker crash) intercepts further taps so report-police was not reachable.
Could not validate from /map because the LogBox red error overlay blocks tab bar taps. Tabs are visible in the DOM but click() timed out on all four tabs.

issues: Top glass header renders correctly with title 'Map', subtitle 'DemoDriver · 0 drivers · 0 alerts' and refresh-btn.
VoiceFAB (bottom-left) and report-fab (bottom-right) sit ~120px above the tab bar -> no overlap with the tab bar (good).
Below the header the area is filled with Google's white error card instead of the satellite imagery -> dramatic visual regression vs the previous SVG map.

action_items: PRIMARY: Enable 'Maps JavaScript API' for the Google Cloud project that owns key AIzaSyDLysQuU6tOcmbdlsHJPYUqviq4TrwEUx0 (Cloud Console -> APIs & Services -> Library -> Maps JavaScript API -> Enable). The key also needs HTTP-referrer authorization for https://motorist-hub.preview.emergentagent.com/* and the project must have a billing account attached.

Create a real Map ID in Cloud Console (Map Management) configured as VECTOR with hybrid type, then put that Map ID into mapId prop in /app/frontend/src/ConvoyMap.web.tsx. Without a valid vector Map ID, AdvancedMarker crashes and tilt/colorScheme are ignored.
Alternative if Map ID isn't possible quickly: replace AdvancedMarker with the classic <Marker> from @vis.gl/react-google-maps and drop mapId/tilt/colorScheme to use a raster map cleanly.

After fixing the above, re-run the regression: login -> /map -> verify .gm-style present + at least one https://maps.gstatic.com or khms tile loaded -> tap report-fab -> tap report-police -> verify a new AdvancedMarker added and 'X alerts' counter increments -> verify tab navigation Talk/Drive/Music/Hub still works.
critical_code_review_comments: ConvoyMap.web.tsx lines 40-46: mapId='convoy-driver-map' is a placeholder string, not a real Cloud-registered Map ID. AdvancedMarker REQUIRES a valid mapId; otherwise AdvancedMarker throws and tilt/colorScheme become no-ops on the raster fallback. This is the root cause of the secondary crash even after enabling the API.

ConvoyMap.web.tsx line 7: KEY is read at module-load time via process.env. With Metro this is fine since EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY is inlined at build time, and we confirmed the key is being sent on the Maps JS request. So the key plumbing is correct - the failure is purely API-not-activated on the Google Cloud side.

ConvoyMap.web.tsx uses raw <div>/<span> inside AdvancedMarker - this is correct for the .web file because @vis.gl/react-google-maps renders into the DOM, but it should NOT appear in ConvoyMap.tsx (native), which it doesn't - good split.
map.tsx geolocation fallback to 37.7749,-122.4194 after 4s timeout works as expected in headless browser (denied geolocation -> fell back, 'DemoDriver · 0 drivers · 0 alerts' rendered).

success_rate.frontend: Login OK, map mount OK, Google Maps tile rendering FAILS (ApiNotActivatedMapError). ~30% of map flow validated.

context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent enables Maps JavaScript API and creates a vector Map ID, re-run /map flow with mobile viewport 375x667. Look for: (1) .gm-style div mounted, (2) at least one img with src containing 'maps.gstatic.com' or 'khms' or 'maps.googleapis.com/maps/vt', (3) no LogBox red overlay, (4) [data-testid=report-fab] tappable -> [data-testid=report-police] tappable -> alerts counter increments, (5) tab bar testIDs (map/talk/drive/music/hub) clickable. Login: demo@revradar.app / demo1234. URL: https://motorist-hub.preview.emergentagent.com
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: The Google Maps JavaScript API is not enabled on the Google Cloud project that owns the API key. Console error: 'Google Maps JavaScript API error: ApiNotActivatedMapError'. This causes the Map component to render Google's generic grey error card instead of map tiles, and prevents any markers/interaction from working. SECONDARY CAUSE: ConvoyMap.web.tsx passes mapId='convoy-driver-map' which is NOT a Cloud-registered Map ID. AdvancedMarker requires a valid vector Map ID and crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading get)' when it isn't valid; this also forces the map into raster mode where tilt and colorScheme are ignored (warning logged). REPRODUCTION: open https://motorist-hub.preview.emergentagent.com -> login demo/demo1234 -> /map -> observe Google's 'Oops! Something went wrong' card and red LogBox error overlay. MITIGATION: (1) In Google Cloud Console enable 'Maps JavaScript API', attach billing, add referrer https://motorist-hub.preview.emergentagent.com/* to the key restrictions; (2) create a vector Map ID and put it in mapId; OR replace AdvancedMarker with classic Marker and remove mapId/tilt/colorScheme.
